paper_title: Unified gas-kinetic wave-particle methods V: diatomic molecular flow

methods.description: The UGKWP method improves the UGKP method by evaluating the free transport terms in numerical flux analytically and sampling only collisionless hydro-particles. This method is used to simulate diatomic molecular flow.

steps.description: Sample the particle quantities (m_k, x_k, v_k, e_k, ω_k, κ_k) by Eq.(26) and Eq. (27) for each newly added collisionless hydro-particle P_k from the hydro-quantities e^(-∆t/τ) W_h.
steps.searchable_summary: Sample particle quantities for collisionless hydro-particles
outputs: particle quantities (m_k, x_k, v_k, e_k, ω_k, κ_k)

steps.description: Sample free streaming time t_f,k by Eq. (24) for particles P_k from W_p. Classify particles into collisionless and collisional particles, then stream all particles by Eq. (28).
steps.searchable_summary: Sample free streaming time and classify particles

steps.description: Calculate the net free streaming flow W_fr by Eq. (29), and evaluate the equilibrium flux F_eq and free transport flux F_h_fr,s by Eq.(18) and Eq.(33), respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate net free streaming flow and evaluate fluxes

methods.method_name: Rykov Kinetic Model for Diatomic Gas
methods.description: The Rykov kinetic model describes the behavior of diatomic gases with translational and rotational degrees of freedom, focusing on the distribution function and its relation to macroscopic variables.

steps.description: Re-write the Rykov kinetic model equation in a more convenient form.
Construct the UGKP method on a discretized physical space and time, defining cell averaged conservative variables.
steps.searchable_summary: Construct UGKP method on discretized space and time

steps.description: Calculate the equilibrium flux term F_eq,s analytically and the free streaming flux term F_fr,s using simulating particles.
steps.searchable_summary: Calculate equilibrium and free streaming flux terms
